title: helicteres semiglabra
intro: Helicteres semiglabra, also known as the rough-fruited trumpet vine or the rough-fruited helicteres, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Helicteres semiglabra include its potential in treating digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used, often in the form of decoctions, infusions, or topical applications, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
